firm.name: SOVADIS
firm.oneLiner: SOVADIS is an M&A advisory firm focused on selling, buying, valuing, and succession planning for Swiss SMEs, especially in the Mittelstand.
fitNotes: Clear fit for an AI-native deal platform because they run structured SME sale and succession processes.
They explicitly mention secure data rooms and due diligence, which suggests document-heavy transactions.
They rely on databases, direct outreach, and network-based buyer search, so workflow tooling could help.
No client portal is stated, so there is no evidence of an existing digital deal platform.

people.name: Reto Schoch

footprint.title: Company leadership
footprint.summary: Named as the managing director and public face of SOVADIS.

footprint.title: Succession expert and owner
footprint.summary: Described as "Nachfolge-Experte & Inhaber" on the succession advisory page.

likelyPriorities: Inference: efficient buyer sourcing and qualification
Inference: structured process support from teaser to closing
Inference: confidentiality and secure document handling

signals.title: Published 6-step succession process
signals.detail: The site lays out a six-phase process from preparation to handover.

signals.title: Published 6-step sales process
signals.detail: The site describes a six-step company sale process including preparation, marketing, negotiations, due diligence, and handover.

signals.title: Reference projects page
signals.detail: The firm publishes selected M&A reference projects and mandates.

signals.title: Interview and talks listed
signals.detail: The homepage lists an interview with Reto Schoch and video talks on succession topics.

play.angle: Lead with the fact that SOVADIS publishes a structured six-step sales and succession process, because that signals they already think in stages, handoffs, and controlled information release. Position Fintalo as a way to make that process lighter, especially around buyer management, document release, and keeping diligence organized without email and Excel sprawl. Avoid generic AI hype or a broad CRM pitch, and do not lead with automation for its own sake, they will care more about confidentiality, process control, and speed to qualified buyers.

objections.objection: We already have a clear process, so we do not need another platform.
objections.counter: That is exactly the point, Fintalo is meant to support an existing process, not replace it. It helps run the same stages with less manual coordination and fewer dropped threads.

objections.objection: Our deals are confidential, so we are cautious about new software.
objections.counter: Fintalo is built around secure collaboration and stage-gated document release, so you can control who sees what and when. It is designed to reduce risk, not add exposure.

objections.objection: We are a small advisory team, so we do not want something heavy to implement.
objections.counter: That is fair, and the goal would be a short demo to see whether it actually simplifies your workflow. If it does not save time quickly, it is probably not worth pursuing.

openingEmail.subject: A simpler way to run your six-step M&A process
openingEmail.body:
Hi Reto,

I saw that SOVADIS publishes a six-step company sale process. Firms with a clear process like that usually still end up juggling buyer lists, document sharing, and diligence updates across email and Excel.

Fintalo is an AI-native M&A operating system that helps advisors manage NDA workflows, buyer/investor coordination, stage-gated document release, and deal collaboration in one place.

Would you be open to a short demo to see whether it could fit the way SOVADIS already works?

Best,
openingEmail.groundedFact: SOVADIS publishes a six-step company sale process.
